macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Nechemia: Scope of Order Requirements (Seder Me'akev)

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Debate: Where Order Matters According to Each View
The Question of Shirayim (Remnants): Is Pouring the Remnants Me'akev on Yom Kippur?
Rabbi Chanina's Statement: Ketoret Done Before Shechitas Par is Disqualified
Clarification: What Gets Disqualified When Order is Violated
Gemara's Challenge: Does Rabbi Yehuda Care About Order of External Actions Done for Internal Purpose (Tzorech Pnim)?
Resolution: Even Rabbi Yehuda Agrees Order Matters for Acts Done for the Sake of the Inner Sanctum

macro_segments.title: Order and Interruption: When Blood Spills, Must One Restart from the Beginning or from the Unit's Beginning?

micro_segments.title: Mishna: If Blood Spills Before Completing Inner Actions, Bring New Blood and Restart from the Beginning
Gemara's Challenge: If Order of External Acts Matters, Why Not Require Repeating Ketoret?
Gemara's Answer: The Mishna Assumes External Acts Don't Need Repetition; Focus is on Blood Only

macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Nechemia: Scope of Order Requirements (Seder Me'akev) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Ulla's Parallel Statement: Shechitas Se'ir Before Blood of Par is Disqualified

macro_segments.title: Order and Interruption: When Blood Spills, Must One Restart from the Beginning or from the Unit's Beginning? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Parallel Problem with Se'ir: If You Did Se'ir Before Par's Blood, Why Not Require New Se'ir?
Ulla's and Rav Afis's Resolution: Discussion Applies Only to Heichal, Not Kodesh Kodashim

macro_segments.title: Units of Atonement: Each Location is a Separate Unit with Its Own Restart Point

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Each Location (Kodesh Kodashim, Heichal, Mizbeiach) is a Separate Unit of Kapara
When Blood Spills Within a Unit: Restart from the Beginning of That Unit Only
When Blood Spills Between Units: No Problem, Simply Begin the Next Unit

macro_segments.title: The Pesukim and Their Meaning: From Ritual Cleansing of the Mikdash to Personal Sin Atonement

micro_segments.title: Pesuk Structure: Multiple Statements of Kippur (Vechipper) for Different Locations and Groups
Gemara's Interpretation of Pesukim: Kodesh Kodashim, Heichal, and Mizbeiach Listed Separately
Peshat vs. Chazal: Peshat Suggests Ritual Cleansing; Chazal Shift to Personal Sin Atonement
Distinction Between Par (Kohanim Cleansing) and Se'ir Le'Azazel (Israel's Sins)
Rabbi Yehuda's View: Se'ir Le'Azazel Atones for All Other Sins (Sha'ar Averot) for Both Kohanim and Israel
Rabbi Shimon's View: Par's Viduy Atones Kohanim for Other Sins; Se'ir's Viduy Atones Israel
The Shift in Emphasis: From Ritual Blood (Dam) to Confession (Viduy) as Primary Vehicle of Atonement

macro_segments.title: Units of Atonement: Each Location is a Separate Unit with Its Own Restart Point (continued)

micro_segments.title: Debate: Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Shimon Say Pick Up Where You Left Off, Not Restart Unit
Consensus: Pouring of Remnants (Shirayim) at Base of Altar is Not Me'akev

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Restarting vs. Continuing: Is It One Animal/One Process or One Sprinkling Process?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Both Sides in Debate Derive Position from Single Pesuk Using Different Interpretations
Rabbi Meir's Position: Chatat Achat Means One Sin Offering—Cannot Use Blood of Two Animals
Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Shimon's Position: Chituy Echad Means One Sprinkling Process—Must Continue Where Left Off
Parallel Case of Log Shemen: Same Debate Over Whether to Restart or Continue
Baraita on Metzora's Oil: Same Positions—Restart at Unit Beginning vs. Continue from Spill Point
Why Pouring on Head is Not Me'akev: It is Shirei Shirayim (Leftovers of Leftovers)

macro_segments.title: Case of Asham Metzora: The Paradox of One Animal and Spilled Blood

micro_segments.title: Scenario: Blood of Asham Spills Before Placement on Ears/Thumb/Toe
Rabbi Meir's Position: Can Bring Another Asham and Restart from Beginning
Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Shimon's Position: Want to Continue from Spill Point but Cannot—Stuck (Ein Lo Takana)
Rav Chisda's Challenge: If Pesuk Says Oto (It), Even Meir Should Be Limited to One Asham
Resolution via Braita: Animal Becomes Olah for Mizbeiach; Need Separate Asham to Complete Process

topical_tags.term: Tzorech Pnim (Done for Sake of Inner Sanctum)
Log Shemen (Measure of Oil for Metzora)
